channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Several of Trump's Cabinet picks — and Trump himself — have been accused of sexual misconduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — While Matt Gaetzhas withdrawn from the nomination process for attorney general, President-elect Donald Trump has picked several other people for his Cabinet and key staff positions who have been accused of some form of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaetz withdraws as Trump's pick for attorney general, averting confirmation battle in the Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Matt Gaetz withdrew Thursday as President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general amid continued fallout over a federal sex trafficking investigation that cast doubt on his ability to be confirmed as the nation's chief federal law enforcement officer.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats advance 5 more Biden judicial nominees
item.description: Senate Democrats advanced five more Biden judicial nominees on Thursday amid a mad dash to confirm left-leaning judges before President-elect Trump assumes office in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Matt Gaetz withdraws from attorney general nomination
item.description: Former Florida congressman Matt Gaetz said he is no longer seeking the position of U.S. attorney general Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk's budget crusade could cause a constitutional clash in Trump's second term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Elon Musk first suggested a new effort to cut the size of government, Donald Trump didn't seem to take it seriously. His eventual name for the idea sounded like a joke too.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate showdown: GOP secures deal with Schumer to save coveted appellate judges for Trump
item.description: Senate Democrats and Republicans struck a midnight deal that would secure four crucial appellate court judge nominations for President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrat Tammy Baldwin details recipe for running in a swing state after victory in Trump-won Wisconsin
item.description: Wisconsin Sen. Tammy Baldwin managed to win re-election as a Democrat while Republican President-elect Donald Trump flipped her state red. Here’s how she thinks she pulled it off.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden has become notably quiet after the 2024 election and Democrats' loss
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has been notably quiet since the Democrats' gut-wrenching defeat at the polls.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson declares support for banning Sarah McBride's access to women's restrooms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson declared Wednesday that lawmakers and staff will have to use the restroom corresponding with their biological sex, a statement directed at Sarah McBride, the first transgender person to be elected to Congress, months before she is set to arrive on Capitol
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-abortion groups have a to-do list for Trump
item.description: With Trump set to begin another term and Republicans in control of Congress, the anti-abortion movement is hoping to continue the gains it made during his first time in office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: Senate Democrats ask FBI for files on Gaetz sex trafficking probe
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee have sent a letter to FBI Director Christopher Wray asking for “the complete evidentiary file” from the bureau's investigation into allegations of sex trafficking of minors against former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Cabinet picks give some of his Muslim voters pause
item.description: Muslims for Trump co-founder Rabiul Chowdhury said some of the nominations are concerning for voters.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Texas offers Trump 1,400 borderland acres for deportation plans
item.description: Texas Land Commissioner Dawn Buckingham offered 1,402 acres in Starr County to the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries: Republicans don't have a mandate
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Tuesday rejected the idea that voters delivered an Election Day mandate to Donald Trump and the Republicans, arguing that the GOP’s razor-thin majority in the House means that nothing will get done in Washington without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s vows for revenge take on new seriousness
item.description: President-elect Trump’s vows to seek revenge have brought a renewed sense of alarm to those who have crossed ways with him now that he’s returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Indigenous journalists question the accuracy of poll reported by some media organizations
item.description: An exit poll by Edison Research included a data point that 65 percent of American Indians supported Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied long-range missiles to strike deeper inside Russia. The move eases limitations on the weapons as Russia deploys thousands of North Korean troops to reinforce its war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg called to apologize for saying bakery denied order over politics
item.description: New York City politicians are calling on Whoopi Goldberg to apologize for suggesting on air that a local bakery had declined to make “The View” co-host a batch of desserts for her birthday because of her political beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida GOP chair approves Gaetz AG nomination; state democratic chair concerned
item.description: There are increasing questions from political figures surrounding President-elect Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Justice Department. However, former Rep. Matt Gaetz is getting support from Evan Power, the chair of the Florida GOP.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
